post.subject: Anyone?
post.body: I fucking love being here, new life, new start, new friends, no parents and time to be independent and discover what i want to do with myself; live my life exactly how i want to and do whatever the fuck i want and have no one stop me. Though there is the odd time of loneliness, such as weekends. I dont want to do home every weekend because i like being on my own, i.e. away from my parents. I know it sounds like a bit of a contracdiction. But i know that if i go home every weekend i will just sit around at home and be bored... maybe it is a little better than being bored here with no one to talk to at all, but i dont have the freedom to do whatever the fuck i want when i go home. Yeah this is very strange i know... unless you do understand. So last weekend i didnt go home and did spend a hell of a lot of time alone. On Friday night i was working until 1am. When i got home made some food and began watching Pirates of the Caribbean all night. I slept until saturday evening then i went to the cinema that evening. After the cinema I got some alcohol started drinking when I got back to my room. Alone. Obviously not something im going to make a habit of. I did get a little drunk, was listening to great music all night that made me feel really good. But i was lonely. I desperately wanted to talk to someone. Desperately wanted the internet so i could sit on msn. Sent a txt or two, with no luck in getting any replies. Generally feeling pretty depressed. Thinking of the few people that are close to me in my life, i mean, 'what-the-fuck'?? I often feel that no one will ever be able to understand me. I am too jaded. I find it hard to converse with people because everything is a cliché and it annoys me. I all too often sit in a crowded room and say nothing because whenever someone does say something its always something stupid, that i probably could have said but thought it wasnt worth while. As Marilyn Manson said, "There's nothing left to say anymore, everything's been said before." Okay so that's meant in a different context, but its the same idea. It does often come to my mind that i could be anyone. Not to be fake or artificial, but when meeting someone for the first time i could be wild and crazy and say all manner of things that to other people may seem out of character, but i find it hard to do that. I have met a lot of new people lately, but havent been as different as i would have liked to have been. Yeah, everyone says 'just be yourself' but when yourself doesnt get you anywhere you want to try something different. I try, and am gona try harder. If it doesnt work then i'll just go back to my usual silent, pensive, people-watching, forward-thinking, always thinking, never-talking sorta guy. I'll let you know how I get on.
